title: Factoring quadratics with a common factor
description: Practice factoring quadratics by first pulling out a common factor so the result looks like a(x+b)(x+c).
topic_path: Get ready for polynomial operations and complex numbers
Factoring quadratics with a common factor

hints:
First, let's factor out the greatest common factor. If the leading coefficient is negative, we'll also factor out $-1$.

The result will be something like this, where the blanks are coefficients:

$_{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\!(_{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\! x^2+\, _{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\! x+\, _{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\!)$

Then, we can try to factor the sum like this:

$_{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\!(_{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\! x+\, _{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\!) (_{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\! x+\, _{\large\llcorner\!\lrcorner}\!)$

###Factor out a the greatest common factor

The greatest common factor is $6$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}6x^2-18x-60 \\\\
&=6(x^2-3x-10)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Factor $x^2-3x-10$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}6x^2-18x-60 \\\\
&=6(x^2-3x-10) \\\\
&=6(x+2)(x-5)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Answer

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}6x^2-18x-60 \\\\
&=6(x+2)(x-5)
\end{align}$

Then, we can try to factor the sum like this:

###Factor out a the greatest common factor

The greatest common factor is $7$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}7x^2+28x-35 \\\\
&=7(x^2+4x-5)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Factor $x^2+4x-5$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}7x^2+28x-35 \\\\
&=7(x^2+4x-5) \\\\
&=7(x-1)(x+5)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Answer

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}7x^2+28x-35 \\\\
&=7(x-1)(x+5)
\end{align}$

###Factor out a the greatest common factor

The greatest common factor is $3$. Since the leading coefficient is negative, we'll factor out $-3$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}-3x^2+6x+9 \\\\
&=-3(x^2-2x-3)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Factor $x^2-2x-3$

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}-3x^2+6x+9 \\\\
&=-3(x^2-2x-3) \\\\
&=-3(x+1)(x-3)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 2]]

###Answer

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}-3x^2+6x+9 \\\\
&=-3(x+1)(x-3)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 3]]

###Factor out a the greatest common factor

The greatest common factor is $2$. Since the leading coefficient is negative, we'll factor out $-2$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}-2x^2+20x-48 \\\\
&=-2(x^2-10x+24)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Answer

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}-2x^2+20x-48 \\\\
&=-2(x-4)(x-6)
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 3]]
